title: erycibe oligantha
intro: Erycibe oligantha, also known as the Brazilian pepper tree or poison pepper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Erycibe oligantha include the ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, although caution should be exercised due to its toxic compounds.
